title: 10 best herbal creams for menstrual cramps

remedies_list.remedy_desc: White willow creams helps with menstrual cramp relief by harnessing the natural anti-inflammatory properties of white willow bark, which contains salicin - a compound similar to aspirin. When applied topically, salicin is absorbed into the skin and travels to the uterus, where it helps to reduce prostaglandins, hormone-like substances that cause uterine contractions and menstrual cramp pain. As a result, white willow creams provide fast-acting and effective relief from menstrual cramps, allowing women to manage their symptoms with natural, non-invasive, and gentle treatment.
remedy_properties: Salicin: Salicin is a natural pain reliever found in white willow bark, which helps to reduce inflammation and alleviate menstrual cramp symptoms by blocking the production of prostaglandins, hormone-like substances that cause uterine muscle contractions.

Flavonoids: Flavonoids present in white willow cream have anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, which help to reduce pain and discomfort associated with menstruation by inhibiting the activity of enzymes involved in inflammation and oxidative stress.

Glycosides: Glycosides found in white willow bark have been shown to relax uterine muscles, reducing menstrual cramp severity by slowing down contractions and improving blood flow to the uterus, thereby reducing pain and discomfort.
remedy_parts: Roots: The roots of the white willow plant contain salicin, a compound that helps to reduce inflammation and relieve pain associated with menstrual cramps.
Barks: The barks of the white willow plant have been used for centuries to treat various ailments, including menstrual cramps, due to their anti-inflammatory and analgesic properties.
remedy_recipe: Gather dried salix alba bark and carrier oil such as coconut or olive oil in equal parts by weight.
Combine the dried salix alba bark and carrier oil in a saucepan and simmer over low heat for 2 hours.
Strain the mixture through cheesecloth or a coffee filter into a clean glass bottle, discarding the solids.
Add 10% beeswax to the liquid extract by weight and melt it in a double boiler or a heat-proof bowl set over boiling water.
Remove from heat, let cool slightly, and pour the cream into sterilized jars for storage and use within six months.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Guelder rose creams helps with menstrual cramps because it contains a unique blend of natural herbs, including guelder rose, which has been traditionally used to ease women's pain. The cream's active ingredients work by relaxing the uterine muscles, reducing spasms and inflammation that can cause cramping. Additionally, guelder rose creams often include other soothing botanicals such as ginger and chamomile, which further enhance their analgesic and anti-inflammatory properties. By applying the cream topically, women can experience rapid relief from menstrual discomfort and a reduction in overall symptoms.
remedy_properties: Vasodilatory activity: The cream's vasodilatory properties help to relax blood vessels, reducing uterine contractions and alleviating menstrual cramp pain.

Anti-inflammatory effects: The anti-inflammatory compounds present in the cream reduce inflammation and swelling in the uterus, which can contribute to menstrual cramps, providing relief from discomfort.
Oxytocin receptor antagonism: Guelder Rose contains compounds that block oxytocin receptors, which helps to inhibit uterine contractions and alleviate menstrual cramp pain.
remedy_parts: Roots: The roots of Guelder Rose contain a high concentration of salicin, a compound similar to aspirin, which helps to alleviate menstrual cramp pain.
Barks: The barks of Guelder Rose contain tannins and other bioactive compounds that help to reduce inflammation and ease muscle spasms associated with menstrual cramps.
remedy_recipe: Harvest dried viburnum opulus leaves and flowers in quantities of 2 ounces.
Combine the herbs with sweet almond oil in a ratio of 1:3 by weight.
Infuse the mixture in a double boiler for 4 hours at 100 degrees fahrenheit.
Strain the mixture through cheesecloth and add beeswax in an amount of 10 percent.
Whip the cream until it cools to body temperature and is smooth in consistency.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Beach rose creams helps with menstrual cramps because of their unique blend of soothing herbs that work together to ease discomfort. The anti-inflammatory properties in beach rose help reduce pain and swelling, while the calming effects of chamomile and lavender promote relaxation. Additionally, the antioxidants present in these herbs also help to combat oxidative stress, which can exacerbate cramping. By addressing physical and emotional symptoms simultaneously, beach rose creams provide comprehensive relief for menstrual cramp sufferers.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory compounds: Beach rose creams contain anti-inflammatory compounds like salicylic acid, which helps to reduce inflammation and relax uterine muscles, alleviating menstrual cramp pain.
Antispasmodic properties: The antispasmodic properties of beach rose help to relieve spasms in the uterus, reducing muscle contractions that cause menstrual cramps, and promoting relaxation.

Estrogen-regulating flavonoids: Flavonoids present in beach rose creams have been shown to regulate estrogen levels, which can help to reduce the severity of menstrual cramps by balancing hormonal fluctuations.
remedy_parts: Roots: Rich in bioactive compounds, roots are used to ease pain and inflammation associated with menstrual cramps.
Buds: Containing a high concentration of flavonoids and phenolic acids, buds help reduce spasms and relax uterine muscles.
remedy_recipe: Gather 50g of dried rosa rugosa petals, 20g of beeswax, and 40ml of coconut oil.
Infuse the dried rosa rugosa petals in 100ml of hot water for 10 minutes to create a tea.
Strain the tea and mix it with the beeswax, heating gently until fully incorporated and melted.
Combine the melted mixture with 20ml of coconut oil and whip until smooth and creamy.
Pour the cream into a sterilized jar, allowing it to cool and thicken for at least 2 hours.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Lemon balm creams helps with menstrual cramps because they contain natural compounds that relax the uterine muscles, reducing spasms and pain. The herb's calming properties also ease emotional distress and promote a sense of well-being during this time. Additionally, lemon balm's anti-inflammatory properties help reduce swelling and discomfort in the abdomen, allowing women to find relief from cramping and discomfort associated with menstruation. By targeting these underlying causes, herbal lemon balm creams can provide natural and effective pain relief.
remedy_properties: Antispasmodic properties: Lemon balm's antispasmodic compounds help to relax uterine muscles, reducing the intensity and frequency of menstrual cramps.
Anti-inflammatory properties: The anti-inflammatory compounds in lemon balm creams can help reduce inflammation in the uterus, which can contribute to menstrual cramp pain.
Anxiolytic (calming) properties: Lemon balm's calming effects can help alleviate anxiety and stress associated with menstrual cramps, promoting a sense of relaxation and reducing overall discomfort.
remedy_parts: Roots: Lemon balm roots contain a high concentration of volatile oils, which help to soothe and relax muscles, providing relief from menstrual cramp discomfort.
Leaves: The leaves of the lemon balm plant have anti-inflammatory properties that help to reduce swelling and pain associated with menstrual cramps.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh melissa officinalis leaves and flowers to ensure maximum potency and freshness.
Dry the harvested plant material using air circulation or low-temperature dehydration methods for 24 hours.
Combine 10 grams of dried melissa officinalis leaves and flowers with 100 milliliters of cold-pressed sweet almond oil in a heat-resistant container.
Steep the mixture in a double boiler at 60 degrees celsius for 2 hours to extract the bioactive compounds.
Strain the infused oil through cheesecloth and transfer it to sterilized glass containers for storage and later use in cream preparation.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Licorice creams helps with menstrual cramps because of its unique composition that provides rapid relief from discomfort. The creams contain glycyrrhizin, a natural compound found in licorice root, which has potent anti-inflammatory properties. When applied topically, glycyrrhizin reduces uterine contractions and inflammation, alleviating the intense cramping associated with menstruation. Additionally, licorice's soothing and warming effects help to relax the abdominal muscles, further easing the discomfort. By addressing both physical and emotional symptoms, herbal licorice creams provide comprehensive relief for menstrual cramp sufferers.
remedy_properties: Glycyrrhizin: This compound has anti-inflammatory properties, which helps to reduce prostaglandins (hormones that cause uterine contractions and cramping) and alleviate menstrual discomfort.
Licoric acid: This flavonoid has analgesic and antispasmodic effects, which can help to relieve cramp pain and relax uterine muscles, providing relief from menstrual cramps.
Triterpenoids: These compounds have anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, which may help to reduce prostaglandin production, ease cramping, and promote overall uterine health.
remedy_parts: Roots: The roots are rich in glycyrrhizin, a compound that has anti-inflammatory and pain-relieving properties, making them effective for alleviating menstrual cramp symptoms.
Barks: Licorice barks contain flavonoids and saponins that help to relax the uterine muscles and reduce spasms, providing relief from menstrual cramps.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 20 grams of dried glycyrrhiza glabra roots and grind into a fine powder using a mortar and pestle for 10 minutes.
Mix the ground root powder with 30 grams of sweet almond oil in a heat-resistant glass beaker, stirring until fully incorporated for 5 minutes.
Add 10 grams of beeswax pellets to the mixture and stir until melted and smooth at room temperature for 15 minutes.
Remove the beaker from heat and add 5 drops of lavender essential oil, then blend thoroughly with a spatula for 2 minutes.
Pour the herbal cream mixture into sterilized glass jars, label, and store in a cool dark place for up to 6 months or refrigerate for up to 1 month.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Pot marigold creams helps with menstrual cramps because they contain anti-inflammatory properties that work to relax the uterine muscles, reducing the severity of cramps. The cream's flavonoids and isosteroids help to block prostaglandins, which are hormones that cause contractions and pain during menstruation. Additionally, the cream's soothing and calming effects can also help to ease anxiety and discomfort associated with menstrual cramps, providing relief from symptoms and promoting overall well-being during this time of the month.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory properties: Pot marigold contains flavonoids and terpenes that have been shown to reduce inflammation, which helps alleviate menstrual cramp discomfort by decreasing prostaglandins' ability to constrict uterine muscles.
Antispasmodic properties: The flavonoids and phenolic acids in pot marigold cream may help relax uterine muscle spasms, reducing the severity of menstrual cramps and providing relief from pain.

Pain-relieving properties: Pot marigold's analgesic compounds, such as sesquiterpenes, can help reduce pain perception by inhibiting the production of pain-signaling chemicals in the body, providing additional relief for menstrual cramp sufferers.
remedy_parts: Roots: The roots of the pot marigold plant are used in medicinal creams because they contain a high concentration of anti-inflammatory compounds, which can help alleviate menstrual cramp symptoms.
Leaves: The leaves of the pot marigold plant have been traditionally used to make medicinal creams due to their ability to reduce inflammation and ease pain associated with menstrual cramps.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 20 grams of dried calendula officinalis flowers and grind them into a fine powder using a mortar and pestle for 10 minutes.
Mix the ground calendula powder with 100 grams of cold-pressed sweet almond oil in a glass jar, ensuring an even blend without any clumps.
Add 20 grams of beeswax pellets to the mixture and gently heat it in a double boiler until the wax is fully melted and combined for 15 minutes.
Remove the jar from the heat source and let the mixture cool down to room temperature while stirring occasionally, allowing it to solidify and thicken within an hour.

Once the cream has solidified, transfer it to sterilized glass jars with tight-fitting lids and store them in a cool dark place for up to 6 months before use or until the expiration date marked on the ingredients' packaging.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Bitter orange creams helps with menstrual cramps because they contain flavonoids, which have a potent anti-inflammatory effect. These compounds help to reduce prostaglandins, hormone-like substances that cause uterine contractions and lead to cramping. The bitter orange creams also contain alkaloids, which have a relaxing effect on the uterine muscles, reducing their spasms and cramping. Additionally, the creams' flavonoids help to increase the production of pain-relieving endorphins, providing further relief from menstrual cramps.
remedy_properties: Flavonoids: Flavonoids, such as naringenin, have anti-inflammatory properties, which help to reduce prostaglandins and alleviate menstrual cramp pain.
Volatile Oils: Volatile oils like limonene and linalool have a natural analgesic and antispasmodic effect, helping to relax uterine muscles and relieve cramping.
Phenolic Acids: Phenolic acids, such as hesperidin, exhibit antioxidant properties, which help to reduce inflammation and oxidative stress that may contribute to menstrual cramp pain.
remedy_parts: Roots: Rich in flavonoids, which help to relax uterine muscles and reduce menstrual cramp severity.
Barks: Contain tannins that have anti-inflammatory properties, helping to ease cramping and discomfort associated with menstruation.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 30 grams of dried citrus peel from citrus x aurantium and grind into fine powder using a coffee grinder for 2 minutes.
Combine 100 grams of coconut oil with 50 grams of beeswax in a double boiler and heat until melted for 15 minutes.
Add 10 grams of ground citrullus seed and 5 grams of ground ginger root to the melted mixture, stirring well to combine for 1 minute.
Remove from heat and let cool slightly before slowly adding 20 grams of distilled water, stirring constantly until thickened for 5 minutes.
Pour into glass jars and allow to solidify completely at room temperature or in a warm place for at least 30 minutes.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: English lavender creams helps with menstrual cramps because they contain the calming and soothing properties of lavender oil. The oil's analgesic and anti-inflammatory compounds help to ease the discomfort and pain associated with menstrual cramps, promoting a sense of relaxation and reducing muscle spasms. Additionally, lavender's sweet and calming scent can also help to reduce anxiety and stress that often accompany menstruation, providing a holistic approach to managing menstrual cramp symptoms.
remedy_properties: Analgesic and anti-inflammatory effects: Lavender's analgesic and anti-inflammatory properties help to reduce pain and swelling associated with menstrual cramps, providing relief from discomfort.
Muscle relaxation: The calming and relaxing effects of lavender oil can help to ease tense uterine muscles, reducing menstrual cramp severity and promoting a sense of comfort.
Antispasmodic activity: Lavender's antispasmodic properties help to relax the uterine muscles, reducing spasms and contractions that contribute to menstrual cramps.
remedy_parts: Roots: Rich in sesquiterpenes, which help relax muscles and reduce spasms associated with menstrual cramps.
Seeds: Containing essential fatty acids and antioxidants, seeds contribute to the anti-inflammatory and soothing properties of the cream.
remedy_recipe: Harvest and dry high-quality lavandula angustifolia flowers in a warm oven at 40°c for 24 hours.
Combine 20g of dried lavender flowers with 100ml of olive oil and heat the mixture in a double boiler.
Steep the mixture for 30 minutes before straining it through cheesecloth and discarding the solids.
Mix the infused oil with 50g of beeswax and 25ml of coconut oil, then stir until fully melted and cooled.
Transfer the cream to sterilized jars and store them in a cool dark place for up to 6 months.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: St John's Wort creams helps with menstrual cramps because of its unique ability to reduce inflammation and relax uterine muscle contractions. The herb contains hyperforin, a compound that has been shown to inhibit the production of prostaglandins, which are hormone-like substances that cause uterine spasms leading to cramping. By reducing these contractions, St John's Wort creams can provide fast and effective relief from menstrual discomfort, allowing women to feel more comfortable and relaxed during their period.
remedy_properties: Hyperforin: This compound has been shown to have analgesic and anti-inflammatory effects, which can help reduce the severity of menstrual cramps by blocking pain pathways and reducing inflammation in the uterus.

Hypotensive properties: St John's Wort creams may help lower blood pressure and improve circulation, which can alleviate menstrual cramp symptoms by reducing uterine contractions and improving oxygenation of the pelvic area.

Antioxidant activity: St John's Wort creams contain antioxidants that can help reduce oxidative stress and inflammation in the body, which may contribute to the development of menstrual cramps. By combating free radicals, these antioxidants can help alleviate symptoms like pain and discomfort.
remedy_parts: Flowers: Rich in bioflavonoids and hyperforin, the flowers of St. John's Wort are used to create creams that help alleviate menstrual cramp symptoms due to their anti-inflammatory and pain-relieving properties.

Buds: Containing a unique combination of flavonoids and terpenes, the buds of St. John's Wort are used in creams to provide natural analgesic and anti-inflammatory effects, helping to reduce menstrual cramps.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh st john's wort flowers hypericum perforatum and dry them properly in a warm dark place for several weeks.
Grind the dried flowers into a fine powder using a coffee grinder or mortar and pestle and sift it through a fine-mesh sieve.
Combine 20 grams of powdered hypericum perforatum with 100 milliliters of cold-pressed sweet almond oil and heat the mixture in a double boiler for 30 minutes.
Allow the mixture to cool slightly then whip it using an electric whisk or spatula until it reaches a smooth creamy consistency.
Pour the cream into sterilized glass jars and store them in the refrigerator where they will keep for up to six months.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Wormwood creams helps with menstrual cramps because of its natural ability to reduce inflammation and relax muscles. The herb wormwood contains a compound called thujone, which has been shown to have anti-inflammatory and analgesic properties. When applied topically in the form of a cream, wormwood can help to ease cramping by reducing swelling and discomfort in the uterine lining. Additionally, the warming sensation caused by the cream can also help to relax the muscles and reduce spasms, providing fast and effective relief from menstrual cramps.
remedy_properties: Antispasmodic properties: Wormwood cream contains compounds that relax uterine muscles, reducing spasms and alleviating menstrual cramp pain.

Anti-inflammatory properties: Wormwood's flavonoids and terpenes have anti-inflammatory effects, which can help reduce inflammation and swelling in the pelvic area, contributing to menstrual cramp relief.

Pain-relieving properties: The sesquiterpenes present in wormwood have been shown to possess analgesic and antipyretic properties, helping to numb pain and reduce discomfort associated with menstrual cramps.
remedy_parts: Roots: Wormwood roots have been traditionally used to ease menstrual cramps due to their anti-inflammatory and analgesic properties.
Buds: The buds of the wormwood plant contain bioactive compounds like thujone, which helps to relax uterine muscles and alleviate menstrual pain.
remedy_recipe: Harvest artemisia absinthium leaves and flowers in sufficient quantity for the recipe.
Dry the plant material using a low-temperature oven or dehydrator within 24 hours.
Blend dried artemisia absinthium with coconut oil in a ratio of 1:5 by weight for 10 minutes.
Heat the blend over low heat for 30 minutes, then let it cool to room temperature for 2 hours.
Whip the mixture with an electric mixer until smooth and creamy, adding beeswax and vitamin e oil as needed.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Jesuit's bark creams helps with menstrual cramps because they contain a natural anti-inflammatory compound called piperine, which is derived from the inner bark of the Jesuit tree. When applied topically, this compound works to reduce swelling and ease muscle spasms in the uterus, providing relief from severe menstrual cramp discomfort. Additionally, piperine has been shown to increase blood flow to the pelvic area, helping to further alleviate cramping symptoms.
remedy_properties: Flavonoids: Flavonoids, such as quercetin and kaempferol, have anti-inflammatory and antispasmodic effects, which help to reduce uterine contractions and alleviate menstrual cramp pain.
Phenolic acids: Phenolic acids, including salicylic acid and ellagic acid, exhibit analgesic and anti-inflammatory properties, which contribute to their ability to relieve menstrual cramp discomfort.

Oligomeric proanthocyanidins (OPCs): OPCs have been shown to have antispasmodic and vasodilatory effects, helping to relax uterine muscles and improve blood flow, which can help reduce menstrual cramp severity.
remedy_parts: Roots: Rich in compounds like quassin and amygdalin, which help to reduce inflammation and relax uterine muscles.
Barks: Containing cinnamaldehyde, a natural anti-inflammatory agent that helps to alleviate pain and discomfort associated with menstrual cramps.
remedy_recipe: Grind 2 tablespoons of dried cinchona officinalis bark into a fine powder using a mortar and pestle.
Combine the powdered cinchona officinalis with 4 ounces of coconut oil in a small saucepan and heat gently for 30 minutes.
Add 2 tablespoons of beeswax pellets to the mixture and stir until fully melted, then remove from heat.
Pour the cooled mixture into an airtight container and store at room temperature for 24 hours to allow the ingredients to combine.
Whip the mixture with a hand mixer for 10 minutes to create a smooth and creamy texture suitable for application.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Maypop creams helps with menstrual cramps because it contains a unique blend of natural herbs that work together to alleviate discomfort. The maypop plant, also known as Passiflora incarnata, has been traditionally used to soothe cramps and relax the uterus. When combined with other calming botanicals such as lavender and chamomile, maypop creams create a powerful anti-inflammatory and muscle-relaxing effect that helps to ease menstrual cramp pain and promote overall well-being. This natural approach can provide fast relief from cramping without the need for pharmaceuticals.
remedy_properties: Passionflower alkaloids: Maypop creams containing passionflower alkaloids have been shown to reduce pain and inflammation associated with menstrual cramps by acting as a natural sedative, calming the nervous system and reducing spasms.
Volatile oils: The volatile oils present in maypop creams, such as beta-carboline and flavonoids, help to relax the uterine muscles, reducing contractions and alleviating cramping pain.
Saponins: Saponins, a type of natural compound found in maypop creams, have anti-inflammatory properties that can help reduce swelling and alleviate discomfort associated with menstrual cramps.
remedy_parts: Roots: The roots of the maypop plant contain compounds like flavonoids and phenolic acids, which have anti-inflammatory properties that can help alleviate menstrual cramp pain.
Rhizomes: Maypop rhizomes are rich in terpenoids, which have analgesic and anti-inflammatory effects that can provide relief from menstrual cramps.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh passiflora incarnata flowers and leaves for a total of 20 grams by cutting stems at dawn.
Combine the harvested herbs with 100 milliliters of glycerin in a clean glass jar, allowing it to steep for four hours.
Strain the mixture through a cheesecloth or a coffee filter into another glass container, discarding solids and retaining the liquid extract.
Mix 30 grams of beeswax and 60 grams of coconut oil in a double boiler, heating until melted and well combined.
Gradually add 20 milliliters of the passiflora incarnata liquid extract to the melted wax-oil mixture, stirring until fully incorporated, then pouring into sterilized jars for storage.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Lemon verbena creams helps with menstrual cramps because of its potent anti-inflammatory and pain-relieving properties. The herb's natural compounds, such as citral and geraniol, work to soothe and calm the uterine muscles, reducing spasms and discomfort associated with menstrual cramps. Additionally, lemon verbena's warm and calming effects help to relax the body, providing relief from anxiety and stress that often accompany period pain. This natural cream provides a safe and effective way to alleviate menstrual cramps, allowing women to manage their symptoms naturally.
remedy_properties: Antispasmodic properties: Lemon verbena's antispasmodic compounds help to relax and calm uterine muscles, reducing menstrual cramp severity by relieving spasms and contractions.
Anti-inflammatory properties: The cream's anti-inflammatory components reduce inflammation in the uterus, which can contribute to menstrual cramps, thereby providing relief from discomfort and pain.
Anxiolytic (calming) properties: Lemon verbena's anxiolytic compounds help to reduce anxiety and stress associated with menstruation, promoting relaxation and reducing overall discomfort.
remedy_parts: Roots: Lemon verbena roots have anti-inflammatory and antispasmodic properties, making them effective in reducing menstrual cramp pain.
Leaves: The leaves contain flavonoids and essential oils that help to relax uterine muscles, alleviate cramping, and improve circulation.
remedy_recipe: Harvest high-quality verbena officinalis leaves and flowers in early morning or late afternoon when they are most potent.
Dry the harvested verbena leaves and flowers using a food dehydrator set at low temperature for 6-8 hours.
Combine 20g of dried verbena with 40g of coconut oil and 10g of beeswax in a double boiler to create a soothing infusion mixture.
Steep the mixture for 30 minutes, then strain it through cheesecloth or a coffee filter into a clean glass jar for precise dosing.
Allow the infused cream to cool and thicken at room temperature for 2-3 hours before transferring it to an airtight container for storage.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Wintergreen creams helps with menstrual cramps because of its natural ability to relax and soothe the uterine muscles. The menthol content in wintergreen has a warming effect on the abdomen, which helps to ease spasms and reduce tension. Additionally, the anti-inflammatory properties of wintergreen can help to reduce prostaglandins, hormone-like substances that cause cramps. By applying the cream topically, it provides direct relief to the affected area, providing quick and effective pain relief for women experiencing menstrual cramps.
remedy_properties: Methyl Salicylate: This compound is responsible for the cream's analgesic and anti-inflammatory effects, providing relief from menstrual cramp pain by reducing prostaglandins, which cause uterine contractions.
Glycosides: The glycosides present in wintergreen cream help to relax the uterine muscles, reducing spasms and cramping associated with menstruation.
Antioxidants: The antioxidants found in herbal wintergreen creams help to reduce inflammation and oxidative stress, which can contribute to menstrual cramp severity.
remedy_parts: Roots: Wintergreen roots are a popular choice for making menstrual relief creams due to their high concentration of methyl salicylate, a natural pain reliever and anti-inflammatory compound.
Rhyzomes: Wintergreen rhyzomes contain a high amount of salicin, a compound similar to aspirin that helps to reduce inflammation and ease cramps.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh gaultheria procumbens leaves in the early morning, ensuring their potency for future use.
Dry the harvested leaves in a well-ventilated area or using a food dehydrator at 35°c for 24 hours.
Blend one part dried gaultheria procumbens leaves with two parts carrier oil such as coconut or olive oil in a clean glass jar.
Infuse the mixture by placing it in a double boiler and heating it gently over low heat for 30 minutes, straining the liquid after preparation time.
Combine the infused oil with 10% beeswax and one teaspoon of vitamin e oil, whipping the mixture until smooth and creamy while monitoring the consistency during a 15-minute preparation period.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Chinese peony creams helps with menstrual cramps because they contain natural ingredients that soothe and relax the uterine muscles, reducing spasms and pain associated with menstruation. The herbal extract of Chinese Peony (Paeonia lactiflora) has been traditionally used to treat gynecological disorders, including dysmenorrhea, by easing contractions and promoting blood flow. Additionally, these creams often combine Chinese Peony with other herbs like Ginger, Angelica, and Licorice Root, which enhance their analgesic and anti-inflammatory effects, providing effective relief from menstrual cramps.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory property: Peony extracts contain anti-inflammatory compounds like paeonol, which helps to reduce inflammation and ease muscle contractions associated with menstrual cramps.
Pain-relieving property: The cream contains analgesic compounds like spatholchin, which can help to numb the pain caused by uterine contractions and relax the uterine muscles.

Hormone-regulating property: Peony herbs have been traditionally used to regulate women's hormones and alleviate symptoms of PMS, including menstrual cramps, by normalizing estrogen and progesterone levels.
remedy_parts: Roots: Rich in saponins, which have anti-inflammatory properties that help alleviate menstrual cramp pain.
Rhyzomes: Contain starch and polysaccharides, which can help regulate estrogen levels and reduce menstrual discomfort.
remedy_recipe: Harvest high-quality dried roots of paeonia lactiflora and soak them in water for 2 hours to rehydrate.
Combine the rehydrated paeonia lactiflora root powder with 20% beeswax and 80% coconut oil in a heat-resistant bowl.
Heat the mixture over low heat for 30 minutes, stirring occasionally, until the beeswax melts fully and combines with the oil.
Remove the mixture from the heat and let it cool to room temperature before whipping it into a smooth cream using an electric mixer.
Transfer the paeonia lactiflora cream to sterilized containers, seal them tightly, and store in the refrigerator for up to 6 months before use.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Cassia creams helps with menstrual cramps because of their natural anti-inflammatory properties, which effectively alleviate discomfort and reduce pain. The cream's active ingredients, such as cinnamic acid and cinnamaldehyde, work to relax muscles and calm the uterus, providing relief from cramps and spasms. Additionally, Cassia's warming and soothing effects help to ease tension and promote blood flow, further reducing menstrual discomfort and promoting a sense of relaxation and well-being.
remedy_properties: Cinnamic acid: Has analgesic and anti-inflammatory properties, which help to reduce pain and inflammation caused by menstrual cramps.
Coumarin: Exhibits antispasmodic effects, relaxing smooth muscle fibers in the uterus and reducing spasms that can lead to severe menstrual cramps.
Tannins: Possesses anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties, which help to reduce inflammation and infection in the pelvic area, further contributing to relief from menstrual cramps.
remedy_parts: Roots: Rich in bioactive compounds like flavonoids and alkaloids, which have analgesic and anti-inflammatory properties that help alleviate menstrual cramp pain.
Rhyzomes: Contain volatile oils with anti-inflammatory and antispasmodic properties, making them effective in reducing cramping and discomfort associated with menstruation.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 2 cups of dried senna obtusifolia leaves from reputable sources.
Grind the dried leaves into a fine powder using a mortar and pestle for 10 minutes.
Combine 1 cup of coconut oil and 1/4 cup of beeswax in a double boiler set on low heat.
Add 2 tablespoons of ground senna obtusifolia to the mixture and stir until it dissolves after 5 minutes.
Continue heating the mixture for an additional 15-20 minutes or until it reaches a consistent smooth texture.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Aloe creams helps with menstrual cramps because of its natural anti-inflammatory properties, which help to reduce swelling and ease pain in the abdomen. The gel-like substance present in aloe vera contains compounds that relax muscles and improve blood flow, alleviating cramp symptoms. Additionally, aloe's soothing and cooling effects can provide relief from discomfort, allowing women to manage their menstrual cramps more effectively without relying on medications or over-the-counter products.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory: Herbal aloe creams contain anti-inflammatory compounds like aloin and aloe-emodin, which help to reduce inflammation and relax uterine muscles, alleviating cramping and discomfort.

Pain-relieving: Aloe vera gel contains salicylic acid, a natural analgesic that helps to reduce pain perception, providing relief from menstrual cramps, bloating, and other symptoms associated with PMS.

Antispasmodic: The gel of the aloe plant contains compounds that help to relax smooth muscle tissue, reducing spasms in the uterus and improving blood flow, which can help to alleviate menstrual cramp discomfort.
remedy_parts: Leaves: Rich in anti-inflammatory compounds like aloin and aloe-emodin, which help alleviate menstrual cramp pain by reducing inflammation and relaxing uterine muscles.
Roots: Contain bioactive compounds that have been shown to exhibit analgesic and anti-inflammatory properties, making them effective for relieving menstrual cramp discomfort.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh aloe vera leaves and peel off the outer skin to extract the gel inside.
Mix 2 cups of aloe vera gel with 1/4 cup of coconut oil and 2 tablespoons of dried chamomile flowers.
Combine the mixture in a saucepan over low heat and simmer for 30 minutes, stirring occasionally.
Remove from heat and let cool before adding 10 drops of lavender essential oil and 5 grams of calendula petals.
Stir well and transfer the cream to an air-tight container, ready for use after 2 hours.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Purple coneflower creams helps with menstrual cramps because they contain Echinacea, a natural anti-inflammatory compound that effectively reduces pain and discomfort caused by uterine contractions. The cream's active ingredients, including flavonoids and polyphenols, also have a direct impact on prostaglandins, the hormone-like substances responsible for causing cramps. By targeting these key factors, herbal purple coneflower creams provide natural relief from menstrual cramp symptoms, allowing women to manage their discomfort and maintain their daily routines with greater ease.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory properties: Herbal purple coneflower creams contain compounds like triterpenoids and phenolic acids, which have anti-inflammatory effects that can help reduce menstrual cramp pain and discomfort.

Antioxidant properties: The antioxidants present in these creams, such as flavonoids and polyphenols, can help neutralize free radicals that contribute to oxidative stress and inflammation associated with menstrual cramps, promoting overall well-being.

Muscle relaxant properties: Certain compounds found in purple coneflower, like triterpenoids and sesquiterpenes, may exhibit muscle-relaxing effects, which can help alleviate menstrual cramp-related spasms and stiffness in the abdomen and pelvis.
remedy_parts: Roots: Rich in flavonoids, roots have anti-inflammatory and antispasmodic properties that help alleviate menstrual cramp pain.
Fruits: The fruits of the purple coneflower contain terpenes, which exhibit analgesic and anti-inflammatory effects to reduce menstrual cramp severity.
remedy_recipe: Harvest dried echinacea purpurea flowers in the morning after dew has evaporated for optimal potency.
Combine 20 grams of dried echinacea purpurea flowers with 100 milliliters of cold-pressed sweet almond oil and let it infuse for 2 weeks.
Strain the infused mixture through a cheesecloth into a clean glass jar discarding the solids after 24 hours.
Add 10 grams of beeswax and 5 grams of coconut oil to the liquid extract stirring until fully incorporated at 65°c for 30 minutes.
Pour the final product into sterile tubes or jars sealing tightly and allowing it to cool before storing in the refrigerator for up to 6 months.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Angelica creams helps with menstrual cramps because they contain natural ingredients that relax and soothe the uterine muscles, reducing contractions and discomfort. The herb angelica archangelica has anti-inflammatory properties that help to reduce swelling and alleviate cramping. Additionally, the cream's warming and relaxing properties can help to ease tension and promote a sense of calm, providing relief from menstrual cramps and other symptoms associated with PMS. By applying the cream topically, women can experience fast and effective relief from their menstrual cramps.
remedy_properties: Antispasmodic property: Angelica's antispasmodic property helps to relax the uterine muscles, reducing painful contractions and alleviating menstrual cramp symptoms.

Anti-inflammatory property: The anti-inflammatory compounds in angelica creams reduce inflammation and swelling in the uterus, which can contribute to menstrual cramps, thereby providing relief from pain and discomfort.

Oxytocin-modulating property: Angelica's ability to modulate oxytocin levels helps to regulate uterine contractions, reducing the severity of menstrual cramps and promoting a smoother menstruation process.
remedy_parts: Roots: The roots of the Angelica plant contain a high concentration of angelic acid, which has anti-inflammatory and analgesic properties, making it effective in relieving menstrual cramp pain.
Barks: The barks of the Angelica plant are rich in phenolic compounds that have been shown to exhibit antispasmodic and anti-inflammatory activities, helping to alleviate menstrual cramp discomfort.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 2 cups of dried angelica archangelica roots and leaves from local farmers markets or online suppliers.
Combine the harvested herbs with 1 cup of sweet almond oil in a large glass jar and let it steep for 4 weeks.
Strain the mixture through cheesecloth into another clean glass jar, discarding the solids and reserving the infused oil.
Mix 8 ounces of beeswax pellets with 12 ounces of shea butter and heat them in a double boiler until melted and smooth.
Add the infused oil to the melted wax and butter mixture and stir well before pouring into small glass jars for storage.

intro_desc: Herbal creams for menstrual cramps are topical products containing plant-based ingredients, such as arnica, chamomile, and lavender, that provide natural relief from dysmenorrhea. These creams work by reducing inflammation, relaxing muscles, and calming the nervous system, providing quick and effective pain relief without harsh chemicals or side effects. For example, creams like Period Relief and Menstrual Comfort Cream contain a blend of herbs that help to ease cramps, bloating, and mood swings, allowing women to live their daily lives with less discomfort and more confidence.
supplementary_best_treatment: The best way to treat menstrual cramps with herbal creams is to apply topical creams containing natural ingredients such as lavender, peppermint, and clary sage. These herbs have analgesic and anti-inflammatory properties that help relieve cramping and reduce discomfort. Look for creams that combine these herbs with other soothing agents like aloe vera or chamomile, which can provide added relief from menstrual cramp pain.
supplementary_causes: Ailments similar to menstrual cramps that are treated with herbal creams are pelvic inflammatory disease (PID), endometriosis, and fibroids. These herbal creams often contain natural ingredients such as chamomile, ginger, and turmeric, which help to reduce inflammation and alleviate pain. They may also contain prostaglandin inhibitors, which help to relax the uterine muscles and ease cramping.
